spec_match.rationale: Configures how FedCM discloses origins in the consent dialog and surfaces a risk score when the iframe origin is hidden — aligns with the spec's motivation of giving users a clear signal of who they're sharing credentials with.

interactive.rationale: Multiple toggles, a live mocked chooser, and a risk-score readout that updates per setting; Continue/Cancel buttons in the mock; well above the static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature blurb sells 'configure exactly how FedCM discloses origins in its browser dialog, watch the mock chooser update live, see the risk score climb when an iframe origin is hidden' — page delivers exactly that.

edge_cases.rationale: Multiple configurations are explorable but the risk-score model isn't transparent; authors don't see why a particular toggle adds N points. Add a per-toggle 'risk delta' annotation.

references.rationale: The see-also list now links to the correct ChromeStatus feature 5176474637959168.

design_system.rationale: All colours via CSS variables; .btn-continue / .btn-cancel override background+color explicitly (rule 6); no static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Make the risk score model transparent
openQuestions.detail: The risk score climbs as origins are hidden but the function is a black box. Add a small 'why' tooltip on the score that lists the active penalties (e.g. '+10 iframe origin hidden, +5 IDP origin abbreviated'). Authors using this as a deployment checklist will want to know how to bring the score back down.

openQuestions.title: Add presets matching common embed patterns
openQuestions.detail: The page is open-ended; users picking this up cold don't know which settings combination matches their real deployment. Add presets: 'first-party iframe (foo.com to foo-static.com)', 'third-party widget (news.com to stripe-payments.com)', 'malicious phishing iframe' — each one snapping the toggles to a meaningful baseline so the risk score has context.

summary: Useful interactive builder with the corrected ChromeStatus reference. Remaining follow-ups are exposing the risk-score function and adding presets to anchor the configuration space.
